title: Wrap order lines in a collection type
scenario: A collection wrapper can own a Vec while keeping later invariants and iteration APIs under one type.
instructions: Define OrderLines with a private Vec<OrderLine>, a constructor, len, and is_empty.

hints.body: Keep the Exposing iteration on collection wrappers code active and define OrderLines with a private Vec<OrderLine>, a constructor, len, and is_empty.
Use the Rust API named in this lesson instead of adding a side helper; the previous arc code should stay in the main solution.
One complete solution for Wrap order lines in a collection type is shown below.

completionExplanation: Wrap order lines in a collection type now extends the active Exposing iteration on collection wrappers code with wrap collection. The previous lesson's behavior remains in the main solution rather than an archived module.
